title: Living in Boulder Vlog 7 - 18 - 2017: Roman Roads

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual life update from Boulder, mentioning his old bike was deemed unrepairable and he purchased a new Merrix disc brake bike for $2000. He also discusses his ongoing project tracing Roman roads digitally, which he has been working on intensively over the past few days.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: not worth repairing, needed a replacement
excited about it and expects it to motivate more outdoor riding and fitness
committed to finishing it; copy-paste approach doesn't work well, prefers tracing from scratch
needs money badly; will seek sponsorship before getting a second job because he believes his website is strong enough to attract sponsors

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: He couldn't sleep because he couldn't breathe.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder V-Log for July 18th. It's 1024 in the morning.
It's been a few days since I did a V-Log because I was too busy working on Rome.
Yeah, I think I'll do some screen captures of what I've been doing real quick and throw them in the background of this video.
So, I didn't have the time to upload a video. I was just too busy with this.
But, oh no, my fan just turned on. I was going to do this video without the fan and then it turned on.
I need to do some thing with my cell phone to get it uncorrupted so I can record stuff with my cell phone and not have to deal with this noise, but I'll do that after.
So, last video I was waiting to hear back from my bike and so yesterday they called me back and they said there's no use working on my bike.
It's too old and I just need to get a new bike.
So, I went into the store to try to get a new bike and it was $580.
It was cheap, but they had a deal on an Eddie Merrick's Muro 69 disc brake red.
It was valued at $4,500, but they gave it to me for $2,000 and so I put my $1,000 pedals on.
So, now I have a $5,500 bike and so that'll be on Wednesday after tomorrow.
So, I'll go down in there and it will do a fitting for me and then I guess I'll have a bike after that.
I'll have two bikes, my old road bike and then this one.
So, I can start riding on the Rolos and since I have a nice bike with disc brakes and everything, I should ride it outside.
So, what's probably going to happen is I'll be motivated to ride my bike in my room and I'll get more fit after like a month of that.
Oh, and then I'll be doing, I'll be riding off to do a 20-kilometer time trial because I'll probably plug in bike racer levels to what I'm doing.
Yeah, and that'll give me the, because I'll be more fit, more sober, more satisfied with myself, kind of more avenues of just more stuff doing.
I'll be able to do even more things and not be so focused on my website.
So, at that point, I'll be able to work on service level on my website and seek grant money.
I need money really bad and start doing stuff like that or get a second job.
But, no, I'll start off trying to get sponsorship because I have a really good website, a really good Google Earth thing of my own.
And, yeah, so that's what I've been up to.
I did, I did the baths of, of Agrippa.
I did the Septuazodium of a apartment building.
And then yesterday I was working on the roads.
So, I think I'll just be working on the roads today and tomorrow, or today.
And then I'll get the bike back.
And then, but what I'm doing, Google Earth and Rome and Boulder and all that, that's stuff I can easily do on the bike.
I think the last time I did a video I was saying something about how I was tracing individual cobblestones.
Yeah, that's crazy.
I did do a road section that I had then copied and I'm pasting it.
And then, that, doing it that way isn't so tight.
I mean, just in a couple days I could do all the roads, both the roads and Rome.
But, but doing all the roads and Rome and tracing and buildings and all that stuff is something I can do on the bike.
So, I'm ready to do it because I want some time of not being able to sleep.
And, because I couldn't breathe and so it's time to get back in shape.
Anyways, I think that's enough talking for now.
I'll do the backgrounds of the videos that I'm working on.
Thank you.
